title_en: Structure and Methodology: A Concise and Critical Seerah
title_id: Struktur dan Metodologi: Sirah yang Ringkas dan Kritis
content_en:
Al-Fusul fi Sirat ar-Rasul — which may be translated as 'Chapters on the Biography of the Messenger' — is a condensed seerah work by Ibn Kathir designed to provide a reliable and accessible account of the Prophet's life without the expansive detail of his larger Al-Bidayah wal-Nihayah. It represents Ibn Kathir's effort to distill the essential narrative of the prophetic biography into a manageable and pedagogically useful form.

The work is organized chronologically through the major phases of the Prophet's ﷺ life: his birth and early childhood, the pre-prophetic years, the beginning of revelation, the Meccan period, the Hijrah to Medina, the Medinan years including the major battles and events, and the final illness and death of the Prophet ﷺ. Each section presents the key events with their supporting narrations, accompanied by Ibn Kathir's characteristic commentary on the reliability of sources and the scholarly lessons to be drawn.

A defining methodological feature of Al-Fusul is Ibn Kathir's consistent attention to hadith evaluation. Unlike some popular seerah accounts that present all narrations on equal footing, Ibn Kathir regularly notes when a report is weak (da'if), very weak, or contradicted by stronger evidence. This makes the work more demanding for casual readers but much more valuable for students who want to develop a critically sound understanding of the prophetic biography.

Ibn Kathir draws primarily on the foundational seerah sources — Ibn Hisham's edited version of Ibn Ishaq's seerah, the Maghazi of Waqidi (used cautiously given scholarly reservations about Waqidi's reliability), and the extensive hadith literature in the six canonical collections and Musnad Ahmad. His synthesis of these materials, filtered through his critical methodology, produces a seerah account that is both historically substantive and methodologically responsible.

The relative brevity of Al-Fusul compared to the seerah sections of Al-Bidayah wal-Nihayah makes it well suited as an introductory or intermediate text. Students who have completed Al-Fusul have a solid grounding in the essential narrative of the prophetic biography and the methodological principles used to evaluate seerah sources, preparing them for more advanced study.
content_id:
Al-Fusul fi Sirat ar-Rasul — yang dapat diterjemahkan sebagai 'Pasal-Pasal tentang Biografi Utusan Allah' — adalah karya sirah yang ringkas oleh Ibn Kathir, dirancang untuk menyajikan kisah kehidupan Nabi ﷺ yang dapat dipercaya dan mudah diakses, tanpa perincian yang meluas seperti dalam Al-Bidayah wan-Nihayah yang lebih besar. Karya ini merupakan upaya Ibn Kathir untuk menyarikan narasi inti biografi kenabian ke dalam bentuk yang dapat dikelola dan berguna secara pedagogis.

Karya ini disusun secara kronologis melalui fase-fase utama kehidupan Nabi ﷺ: kelahiran dan masa kecil beliau, tahun-tahun sebelum kenabian, awal wahyu, periode Makkah, hijrah ke Madinah, tahun-tahun di Madinah termasuk perang-perang besar dan peristiwa-peristiwa penting, serta sakit dan wafatnya Nabi ﷺ. Setiap bagian menyajikan peristiwa-peristiwa kunci beserta riwayat-riwayat penopangnya, disertai komentar khas Ibn Kathir tentang keandalan sumber-sumber dan pelajaran ilmiah yang dapat dipetik.

Ciri metodologis yang menonjol dari Al-Fusul adalah perhatian konsisten Ibn Kathir terhadap evaluasi hadis. Tidak seperti beberapa kisah sirah populer yang menyajikan semua riwayat secara setara, Ibn Kathir secara rutin mencatat ketika suatu riwayat berstatus daif, sangat lemah, atau bertentangan dengan bukti yang lebih kuat. Hal ini membuat karya ini lebih menuntut bagi pembaca awam, tetapi jauh lebih berharga bagi para pelajar yang ingin mengembangkan pemahaman kritis yang kokoh tentang biografi kenabian.

Ibn Kathir terutama bersandar pada sumber-sumber sirah yang fundamental — versi editan Ibn Hisham atas Sirah Ibn Ishaq, Maghazi karya al-Waqidi (digunakan secara hati-hati mengingat keberatan ulama terhadap keandalan al-Waqidi), serta literatur hadis yang luas dalam enam koleksi kanonik dan Musnad Ahmad. Sintesisnya atas bahan-bahan ini, yang disaring melalui metodologi kritisnya, menghasilkan kisah sirah yang secara historis bersubstansi dan secara metodologis bertanggung jawab.

Keringkasan relatif Al-Fusul dibandingkan dengan bagian-bagian sirah dalam Al-Bidayah wan-Nihayah menjadikannya sangat cocok sebagai teks pengantar atau menengah. Para pelajar yang telah menyelesaikan Al-Fusul memiliki landasan yang kokoh dalam narasi inti biografi kenabian dan prinsip-prinsip metodologis yang digunakan untuk mengevaluasi sumber-sumber sirah, mempersiapkan mereka untuk kajian yang lebih mendalam.
content_ar:
Al-Fusul fi Sirat ar-Rasul — which may be translated as 'Chapters on the Biography of the Messenger' — is a condensed seerah work by Ibn Kathir designed to provide a reliable and accessible account of النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم's life without the expansive detail of his larger Al-Bidayah wal-Nihayah. It represents Ibn Kathir's effort to distill the essential narrative of the prophetic biography into a manageable and pedagogically useful form.

The work is organized chronologically through the major phases of النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم's صلى الله عليه وسلم life: his birth and early childhood, the pre-prophetic years, the beginning of revelation, the Meccan period, the Hijrah to Medina, the Medinan years including the major battles and events, and the final illness and death of النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم صلى الله عليه وسلم. Each section presents the key events with their supporting narrations, accompanied by Ibn Kathir's characteristic commentary on the reliability of sources and the scholarly lessons to be drawn.

A defining methodological feature of Al-Fusul is Ibn Kathir's consistent attention to hadith evaluation. Unlike some popular seerah accounts that present all narrations on equal footing, Ibn Kathir regularly notes when a report is weak (da'if), very weak, or contradicted by stronger evidence. This makes the work more demanding for casual readers but much more valuable for students who want to develop a critically sound understanding of the prophetic biography.

Ibn Kathir draws primarily on the foundational seerah sources — Ibn Hisham's edited version of Ibn Ishaq's seerah, the Maghazi of Waqidi (used cautiously given scholarly reservations about Waqidi's reliability), and the extensive hadith literature in the six canonical collections and Musnad Ahmad. His synthesis of these materials, filtered through his critical methodology, produces a seerah account that is both historically substantive and methodologically responsible.

The relative brevity of Al-Fusul compared to the seerah sections of Al-Bidayah wal-Nihayah makes it well suited as an introductory or intermediate text. Students who have completed Al-Fusul have a solid grounding in the essential narrative of the prophetic biography and the methodological principles used to evaluate seerah sources, preparing them for more advanced study.
